children.name: Aramus
children.description: A monotypic genus of a long-legged, brown-streaked wading bird with a long, slightly curved bill adapted for extracting snails from their shells. The sole member of its family, it shows features intermediate between rails and cranes.

children.name: Aramus guarauna
children.description: A distinctive wading bird with dark brown plumage heavily streaked with white, a long olive-brown bill and a peculiar limping gait from which it derives its name. It ranges from Florida and the Caribbean through Central America to northern Argentina. It feeds almost exclusively on apple snails, using its specialised bill tip to extract the mollusc from its spiral shell.
